steps.title: Identify what “similarity” means here
steps.content: The student wants to emphasize a similarity between Kate Walker and Georgiana C. Brumfield. From the notes: both were lighthouse keepers, Walker from 1894–1919 and Brumfield from 1895–1919. A correct answer must name both women and highlight something they share — their shared job title and their overlapping years of service.

steps.title: Test each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — Lighthouse keepers during the late nineteenth and early twentieth centuries were crucial to ensuring safe navigation for watercraft.: This makes a general statement about all lighthouse keepers without mentioning either woman by name — it doesn’t establish a similarity between them specifically. ✗
  • Choice B — From 1894 to 1919, the nighttime waters of New York were more navigable thanks to Kate Walker.: This focuses only on Kate Walker; Brumfield is not mentioned, so no similarity is expressed. ✗
  • Choice C — Kate Walker and Georgiana C. Brumfield were both lighthouse keepers during the late nineteenth and early twentieth centuries.: This names both women, identifies their shared occupation (lighthouse keeper), and places them in the same time period — directly emphasizing their similarity using the notes. ✓
  • Choice D — Kate Walker and Georgiana C. Brumfield spent their careers as lighthouse keepers in different lighthouses.: This mentions both women but emphasizes a difference (different lighthouses), not a similarity. ✗
That matches Choice C — Kate Walker and Georgiana C. Brumfield were both lighthouse keepers during the late nineteenth and early twentieth centuries..
